benign.reply: You're doing great, just a tiny tweak. We say "small steps lead to big wins." Your project sounds tough but very important. What's the next step for you and your team?
benign.user_last: the biggest challenge is making the sensors accurate in all conditions, like rain or sand. also, the software needs to process data fast so players get real-time feedback. it's tricky but we're working on it. *chal chal*, small steps!

benign.reply: Good to hear the players were supportive. You said "malfunctioning," that's a strong word. You could also say "acting up" in casual English. For example, "Some sensors kept acting up during the trials." What did you fix first, the hardware or the software?
benign.user_last: trials were ok, but some sensors kept malfunctioning. had to debug a lot. players were patient, though. one player said, "acha, lagta hai ke yeh kaam karega." (it feels like this will work.)

benign.reply: You did great by solving the hardware issue first, very logical. "Stabilized" is a good word to show things became steady. On a different note, what kind of music helps you stay focused while debugging?
benign.user_last: hardware first. the sensors were loose in their mounts, so i redesigned the casing. after that, i updated the code to filter out noise. took a while, but finally everything stabilized.

benign.reply: Music can really help focus. You mentioned instrumental music helps you think, what kind of instrumental do you prefer?
benign.user_last: sometimes, yeah. especially when i need to focus. a good diljit dosanjh song can really keep me going. but if the code gets too tough, i switch to instrumental, helps me think better. you? what's your vibe?
